In just under 2 weeks since launch, the  M1 powered Mac mini has catapulted  Apple to the top rank in the desktop market in Japan by a whopping 14.4%, according to market data by   BCN Retail.

自1900年以来，与父母同住的年轻人比例为29-52％
 在过去的几十年中，从2008年的金融危机到今年的全球大流行，年轻人都面临着严峻的经济现实，这既造成了工作岗位的巨大损失，也造成了金融稳定。
 尽管尚未完全捕捉到COVID-19的广泛影响，但与120年来目睹的年轻人相比，现在的年轻人与父母住在一起的程度更高，甚至超过了大萧条时代。
  今天的年轻人归为“千禧一代”和“ Gen-Zers”。对于他们来说，COVID-19只是他们所面临的财务困境清单中的又一个新事物，例如不稳定的就业市场和生活成本的上升。
 有一些可能的因素可以解释与父母同住的年轻人的增加。
 1.低迷的就业市场拥有多个学位的咖啡师或服务器已成为挣扎的千禧一代的普遍写照。尽管结果不容乐观，但它并不需要尝试。今天的年轻人实际上是历史上受过教育的一代。不幸的是，学位并没有像前几代人一样画出成功的道路。
 2.迟婚如今，人们结婚的时间比以前的历史平均水平晚了将近十年，而且许多年轻人选择与父母同住，直到结婚。还值得注意的是，随着时间的流逝，年轻人的结婚率比过去更低。
note_en:
Since 1900, the portion of young adults living with their parents has ranged from  29-52%
 In the last few decades, young adults have faced harsh economic realities—from the financial crisis in 2008 to this year’s global pandemic, both triggering catastrophic losses in jobs and financial stability.
 And while the widespread effects of COVID-19 have yet to be fully captured, young adults are already now living with their parents to a greater degree than witnessed in  120 years—surpassing even the Depression-era generation.
  Young adults today are categorized as either late Millennials and Gen-Zers. For them, COVID-19 has just been another addition to the list of financial hardships they’ve been up against, such as a precarious job market and the rising cost of living.
 There are a few possible factors that could explain the increase in young adults living with their parents.
 1. The lackluster job market The barista or server with multiple degrees has become a common portrayal of the struggling millennial. Despite the less than rosy outcomes, it has not been for want of trying. Younger people today are actually the  most educated generation in history. Unfortunately, a degree does not map out a path to success the way it did for prior generations.
 2. Tying the knot later Today, people get  married nearly a decade later than prior historical averages, and many young adults are opting to stay with their parents until they tie the knot. It’s also worth noting that as time goes on, young adults are getting married at lower rates than in the past.
title: 现在，超过一半的美国年轻人与父母同住
title_en: Over Half of U.S. Young Adults Now Live with Their Parents

note:
一级方程式赛车几乎没有驶入赛道的三分之一，直到红旗暂时中断了本周末的巴林大奖赛。 F1比赛的开始通常很混乱，尤其是在后半段，因此在周日证明了这一点，当时由罗曼·格罗斯让（Romain Grosjean）驾驶的哈斯VF-20在退出三号弯后就向右偏离轨道。
 与接下来发生的事情不同，第一圈的撞车在这项运动中并不罕见。格罗斯让的汽车以约137英里/小时（220公里/小时）的速度撞上护栏后，突然裂开一半，然后起火，这已经30多年了。更糟的是，前半部分贯穿了护栏，护栏位于这条赛道的两侧。随着撞车事故的发生，这使人想起了F1的旧时代，这种时代夺走了Roger Williamson或Francois Cevert等驾驶员的生命。
 但是格罗斯让（Grosjean）小心翼翼地走开了，伤得比手上的灼伤还多。这证明了现代F1设计的安全性。
   看到现代F1赛车在撞车后会分成两半是不寻常的，在这种情况下，这是设计使然。在F1赛车中，与您驾驶的汽车不同，该汽车的引擎安装在车辆的车厢内，引擎也是螺栓固定在碳纤维单壳底盘上的结构部件。在极端的碰撞中（该碰撞记录超过50G），这些螺栓会折断，让两个半部以各自的方式消散能量。
 但是其他F1赛车手却没有像他们的赛车那样产生高G冲击。周日发生事故的另一个因素是汽车穿透了护栏屏障。当汽车的前部穿通时，硬壳式硬碟在大部分时间内通过，然后停下。尽管单体壳无处可走，但装有引擎，变速箱和电池的汽车后半部（以及大部分）仍在行驶。这就是为什么螺栓会卡住的原因，也是为什么可以在距离赛道几米的地方看到汽车后半部（大部分保持完好无损）的原因。
   接下来发生的是一次震惊-导演被切掉之前，明亮的黄色火焰痛风飞向空中。 （一级方程式赛车在得知格罗斯让的安全后才显示了更多的空难镜头。）上一次F1飞机失火导致起火是在1989年，这应该让您知道这有多寻常。
 那次较早的撞车事故导致了新的安全法规，要求F1汽车使用单体外壳内的凯夫拉尔燃料电池。那是格罗斯让（Grosjean）留下的地方，并由碳纤维隔板保护。但是，燃料必须从燃料电池流到发动机，尽管这些燃料应该使用干式连接器，但很明显，有一些汽油（可能是几公斤）溢出并遇热。
这项运动的技术总监罗斯·布朗（Ross Brawn）称护栏的渗透确实需要进一步分析。这种护栏（也称为Armco）已被更现代的电路保护所取代，例如Tecpro（通常在Formula E和其他F1赛道上看到）或SAFER护栏，该护栏在美国IndyCar和NASCAR中广泛使用。 。
  在每次比赛开始时，一辆医疗车都会追逐这辆车，特别是在发生此类事件时要迅速到达现场。幸运的是，这次撞车事故发生在三号弯之后，当时速度更快的F1赛车有时间抛弃强大的旅行车。坠机事故发生后的30秒内，F1的旅行医务人员伊恩·罗伯茨（Ian Roberts）帮忙将烟熏的格罗让（Grosjean）越过护栏，医疗车驾驶员艾伦·范德默（Alan van der Merwe）用灭火器将他们俩倒下。
  这比F1强制座舱疏散测试的时间期限要长，F1规定驾驶员必须能够在10秒内退出汽车。但是这些测试是在理想条件下进行的，而不是在汽车着火并楔入护栏时进行的。幸运的是，从年初开始，F1对赛车手在比赛中必须穿着的防火服采取了更加严格的要求。
 作为F1赛车手，格罗斯让（Grosjean）穿着长防火内衣，袜子和三层防火工作服下面的巴拉克拉法帽，防火手套和防火鞋，所有这些材料均由芳纶纤维制成，这种纤维难于导热。从今年年初开始，所有这些物品都必须符合FIA 8856-2018的规定，该规定要求每件服装保护穿着者的时间比他们在2019年穿着的同类衣服长约20％。
  在昨天的撞车事故中，最重要的安全装置也许是晕轮装置，该装置于2017年被引入这项运动中，此前驾驶舱驾驶舱的驾驶员遭受了许多严重的头部受伤和致命撞车事故。它看起来像人字拖鞋的上部，从驾驶舱开口的前面发芽，并缠绕在侧面，与驾驶员头部后方的空气箱汇合。
 光环由钛制成，重约20磅（9千克），必须能够承受125kN的冲击而不会失败。当它于2017年推出时，受到了车迷甚至司机的广泛好评，尤其是Grosjean。这主要是因为它的外观。但是光环几乎可以肯定格罗斯让的生命，光环阻止了护栏的顶部与头盔接触，这肯定是致命的。
 确实，格罗斯让本人现在已经is依。在周日晚上从巴林医院的病床上向社交媒体发布的视频中，他告诉追随者：“几年前我不曾参加《光晕》，但我认为这是我们带到一级方程式赛车上的最大乐趣，没有它，我今天将无法与您通话。”这名法国车手是哈斯车队的最后一个赛季，他将参加下周的比赛-也将在巴林的萨克希尔赛车场举行-但可能会在两周后回到阿布扎比的赛季决赛中。
note_en:
The Formula 1 cars had barely made it a third of the way around the track before the red flag brought a temporary halt to this weekend's Bahrain Grand Prix. The start of an F1 race is often chaotic, especially among the back half of the grid, and so it proved on Sunday when the Haas VF-20 driven by Romain Grosjean speared off track to the right after exiting turn three.
 First-lap crashes aren't that unusual in the sport, unlike what happened next. After hitting the guardrail at about 137mph (220km/h), Grosjean's car ripped in half and then burst into flames—something that hasn't happened for over 30 years. To make matters worse, the front half penetrated between the guardrails that lined this section of track. As crashes go, this one was more reminiscent of the bad-old days of F1, the kind that claimed the lives of drivers like Roger Williamson or Francois Cevert.
 But Grosjean walked away—understandably gingerly—with little more injury than some burns to his hands. And that is a testament to the safety that's designed into modern F1.
   While it is unusual to see a modern F1 car split in half after a collision, in this case, that was by design. Unlike the car you drive, which has an engine mounted inside a bay within the vehicle, in an F1 car, the engine is also a structural part that bolts to the carbon fiber monocoque chassis. And in extreme crashes—this one registered over 50Gs—those bolts will break and let the two halves go their separate ways to dissipate energy.
 But other F1 drivers have had high-G impacts without their cars coming apart like this. The added factor in Sunday's crash was the car penetrating the guardrail barrier. As the nose of the car punched through, the monocoque traveled most of the way through and then stopped. While the monocoque had nowhere else to go, the rear half of the car, containing the engine, gearbox, and battery—and therefore most of the mass—was still going. That's why the bolts snapped and why the back half of the car can be seen, mostly intact, a few meters up the track.
   What happened next was a shock—a bright gout of yellow flame soaring into the air before the director cut away. (Formula 1 only showed more footage of the crash after knowing Grosjean was safe.) The last time an F1 crash resulted in a fire was in 1989, which should give you an idea of how unusual this was.
 That earlier crash resulted in new safety regulations that require F1 cars to use  Kevlar fuel cells contained within the monocoque. And that's where Grosjean's remained, protected by a carbon-fiber bulkhead. However, fuel has to get from the fuel cell to the engine, and although these are supposed to use dry-break connectors, it's obvious that some amount of gasoline—probably a few kilograms—was spilled and met something hot.
 The sport's technical director, Ross Brawn,  has called out the penetration of the guardrail as something that definitely requires more analysis. That style of guardrail, also known as Armco, has been superseded by much more modern circuit protection like  Tecpro (commonly seen in Formula E and at other F1 tracks) or the  SAFER barrier, which is widely used in IndyCar and NASCAR here in the US.
  At the start of each race, a medical car chases the pack of cars, specifically to be on site quickly in incidents such as this. And luckily, this crash happened just after turn three, before the much faster F1 cars had time to ditch the powerful station wagon. Within thirty seconds of the crash, a slightly smoky Grosjean was being helped over the guardrail by F1's traveling medic, Dr Ian Roberts, with the medical car driver Alan van der Merwe spraying them both down with a fire extinguisher.
  That's longer than the time limit for F1's mandatory cockpit evacuation test, which states that a driver has to be able to exit their car within 10 seconds. But those tests happen under ideal conditions, not when your car is on fire and wedged in a guardrail. It was fortunate, then, that at the beginning of the year, F1 adopted  stricter requirements for the fireproof clothing that each driver must wear during a race.
 As an F1 driver, Grosjean was wearing long fireproof underwear, socks, and a balaclava underneath three-layer fireproof overalls, fireproof gloves, and fireproof shoes, all made from an aramid fiber called Nomex that is very bad at conducting heat. And from the beginning of this year, all of those items had to conform to FIA 8856-2018, which requires each garment to protect the wearer for about 20-percent longer than the equivalent they wore in 2019.
  Perhaps the most important safety device in yesterday's crash was the halo device, which was introduced to the sport in 2017 after a number of serious head injuries and fatal crashes among drivers in open-cockpit cars. It's the thing that looks like the upper part of a flip-flop, sprouting up from just ahead of the cockpit opening and wrapping around the sides to meet up with the airbox behind the driver's head.
 Made from titanium and weighing about 20lbs (9kg), the halo has to survive an impact of 125kN without failing. It was widely panned by fans and even drivers— particularly Grosjean—when it was introduced in 2017, mostly because of the way it looked. But Grosjean's life was almost certainly saved by the halo, which prevented the top bar of the guardrail from making contact with his helmet, something that would almost certainly have been fatal.
 Indeed, Grosjean himself is now a convert.  In a video posted to social media from a Bahraini hospital bed on Sunday night, he told followers: "I wasn’t for the Halo some years ago but I think it’s the greatest thing that we’ve brought to Formula 1 and without it I wouldn’t be able to speak to you today." The French driver, who is in his last season for the Haas team, will sit out next week's race—also to be held at Bahrain's Sakhir circuit—but may be back in action for the season finale in Abu Dhabi two weeks from now.
title: 罗曼·格罗斯让（Romain Grosjean）如何摆脱数十年来最可怕的F1撞车事故
title_en: How Romain Grosjean walked away from F1’s scariest crash in decades

  现在，由法院决定指控是正确的还是毫无根据的。 “法院可以传唤许愿或提出认罪。我们应该在未来的日子里知道，”法国数字部长塞德里克·奥（CédricO）告诉我。

 如果Wish被判有罪，该公司可能要冒其在法国年收入中高达10％的风险。尤其值得一提的是，看看Wish是否对第三方商家销售的产品负责。
 由于欧洲即将出台的《数字服务法》应从2000年起全面改革电子商务指令，因此案情的时机有点奇怪。所有人的目光都集中在内容审查上，但《数字服务法》也应关注假冒卖家，市场责任等。
note_en:
In addition to those wrongly labeled products, the administration says Wish pretends products are on sale while they aren’t. The platform could be displaying -70%, -80% or -90% on some products even though the original price is completely made up.
 The administration in charge of the investigation is the   direction générale de la concurrence, de la consommation et de la répression des fraudes (DGCCRF), an administration that reports to the French Ministry for the Economy and Finance. They have transmitted  the report to a court in Paris.
  Now, it’s up to the court to decide whether the allegations are right or unfounded. “The court can subpoena Wish or offer to plead guilty. We should know in the coming days,” France’s digital minister Cédric O told me.
 On Twitter, Cédric O highlighted one case in particular. “Wish already stood out during the first lockdown by selling facemasks that don’t meet safety standards. French people who are using the app to find low-cost products should know that they’ll mostly find scams,” he tweeted.
 . @WishShopping s’était déjà distinguée pendant le 1er confinement en vendant des masques ne respectant pas les normes. Les Français-e-s qui utilisent l'application pour y trouver du low-cost doivent savoir qu'ils y trouveront surtout des arnaques.  @alaingriset  @BrunoLeMaire
 If Wish is found guilty, the company could risk up to 10% of its annual revenue in France. In particular, it’s going to be interesting to see whether Wish is responsible for products sold by third-party merchants.
 The timing of the case is a bit odd as Europe’s upcoming Digital Services Act should overhaul the e-commerce directive from 2000. All eyes are on content moderation, but the Digital Services Act should also focus on counterfeit sellers, the liability of marketplaces and more.
title: 法国政府怀疑销售假冒产品的愿望
title_en: French administration suspects Wish of selling counterfeit products

note:
交易细节尚未透露，但消息人士称，交易额在10亿美元左右。 《华尔街日报》今天早些时候发布了该交易的报告。
 Kustomer由首席执行官Brad Birnbaum和Jeremy Suriel共同创立（二者在Airtime和AOL等多个领域合作，并已将前一家初创公司卖给了Salesforce），已从投资者那里筹集了约1.74亿美元的私人资金，其中包括Coatue，Tiger Global Management，Battery Ventures，Redpoint Ventures，Cisco Investments，Canaan Partners，Boldstart Ventures和Social Leverage。根据PitchBook的估计，它的最新估值为7.1亿美元。
  Facebook对Kustomer的兴趣非常简单：该公司一直在缓慢地建立起一个大型企业，为其平台上的企业提供客户服务。
 如今，大约有1.75亿人以这种方式使用Facebook，这包括使用Facebook与以Facebook为主要在线“身份”的企业进行互动的人–如今，他们通常只是拥有自己的网站或移动应用程序而已Facebook上的Page –以及那些在Facebook拥有的消息传递应用程序（如Instagram，Messenger和WhatsApp）上提供对话渠道的企业，以补充其他联系方式（有时是唯一的联系方式）。
 考虑到Facebook拥有超过20亿用户，所以1.75亿听起来并不多。
 但是随着公司逐渐看到来自Snapchat，TikTok等公司的激烈竞争，随着时间的推移，拥有更好的产品来与其他服务一起销售企业，将为Facebook提供一种更好的将其锁定在Facebook生态系统中的方式。这也将使该公司在新的收入来源上更有实力，以补充广告，而广告仍然是其最大的摇钱树。
 确实，客户服务对于Facebook来说是一个有趣的过程。该公司一直在其消息传递应用程序上投资并为企业构建许多附加功能-例如，最近在WhatsApp上，它开始使企业更容易让人们在该应用程序上购物并做更多的事情。在该客户服务中，一个巨大的行业远远超出了Facebook围墙花园。
确实，Kustomer和其他CRM公司用来描述其作用的特定术语是“全渠道”客户关系。也就是说，它为Kustomer业务用户提供了可能与客户进行“对话”的许多不同地方的完整图片-无论是在应用程序，社交媒体，网站，通过聊天机器人或电子邮件等上。逻辑是，这可以提高座席的效率，并让他/她更好地了解业务在这些渠道之间的发展状况，以及有关特定用户从这些渠道之一与公司联系的更多背景信息，以及更完整的信息客户自己的图片。
 对于Facebook而言，到目前为止，其“客户关系”资料是关于其应用程序内的用户的。无论使用哪种平台，这都给它提供了更大的机会，可以从根本上控制更大范围的图片和更大的关系。
 巧合的是，直到本月初，我才报道Snap收购了Voca.ai，后者是客户支持语音机器人。
 虽然我们不知道Snap将如何使用该技术（有人猜测这可能是为其眼镜开发更多的语音命令和基于音频的技术），但我当时写道，将其整合到更大的产品组合，为已经使用Snapchat推销自己的企业提供更多工具。在这方面，收购Kustomer感到非常及时。
note_en:
Terms are not being disclosed but sources are saying it’s in the region of $1 billion. Reports of the deal were published earlier today by  WSJ.
 Kustomer — co-founded by CEO Brad Birnbaum and Jeremy Suriel (the two worked together across a range of other places, including Airtime and AOL and had sold a previous startup to Salesforce) — had  raised around $174 million in private funding from investors that included Coatue, Tiger Global Management, Battery Ventures, Redpoint Ventures, Cisco Investments, Canaan Partners, Boldstart Ventures and Social Leverage. It was last valued at $710 million, according to estimates from  PitchBook.
  Facebook’s interest in Kustomer is very straightforward: the company has been slowly building up a big business providing customer services to businesses on its platform.
 There are some 175 million people using Facebook this way today, covering both those who use Facebook to engage with businesses that use Facebook as their primary online “identity” — in place of a website or mobile app of their own, companies today often simply have a Page on Facebook — and those businesses that provide conversation channels on Facebook-owned messaging apps like Instagram, Messenger and WhatsApp as a complement to other ways (and sometimes the sole way) to contact them.
 Considering that Facebook has upwards of 2 billion users, 175 million doesn’t sound like a lot.
 But as the company starts to see more keen competition from the likes of Snapchat, TikTok and likely others over time, having a better product to sell businesses alongside their other services will give Facebook a better way of locking them into the Facebook ecosystem. It will also give the company a stronger shot at a newer revenue stream to complement advertising, which remains its biggest cash cow by a big margin.
 Indeed, customer service is an interesting play for Facebook to be making. The company has been investing in and building a number of additional features for businesses on its messaging apps — most recently on WhatsApp, for example, it  started to make it easier for businesses let people shop and do more on the app. Within that customer service is a huge industry that stretches well beyond the Facebook walled garden.
 Indeed, the specific term Kustomer and other CRM companies use to describe what it does is “omni-channel” customer relations. That is to say, it gives the Kustomer business users a complete picture of the many disparate places where “conversations” might be happening with customers — be it on apps, on social media, in websites, via chatbots, or email, etc. The logic is that this makes the agent more efficient and gives him/her a better picture of both how the business is faring across those channels, and more context about a specific user contacting the company from one of those channels, as well as a more complete picture of the customers themselves.
 For Facebook, it’s “customer relations” profile up to now has been about users within its app walls. This gives it a much bigger opportunity to essentially control that bigger picture and bigger relationship, regardless of the platform being used.
 Coincidentally, it was only earlier this month that I reported that  Snap acquired Voca.ai, which makes customer support voice bots.
 While we have no idea how Snap will use that tech — some have speculated it could be to build more voice commands and audio-based tech for its Spectacles — I wrote at the time that it would make a lot of sense to bring this into a bigger product portfolio providing more tools to businesses already using Snapchat to market themselves. This Kustomer acquisition feels very timely in that regard.
title: 消息人士称，Facebook确认已收购Kustomer，交易价格为10亿美元
title_en: Facebook confirms it has acquired Kustomer, sources say for $1B

note:
按照FCC的惯例，当政府更换政党时，主席要离开。排接手汤姆惠勒，谁在奥巴马的年年底主持委员会，辞去在特朗普的选举。拜登政府尚未宣布选择该通讯机构的新领导人。
 在FCC的正式备忘录中，Pai感谢他的同事并总结了他执掌四年的成就（必须说，他是亚洲人后裔的第一任专员）：
  在过去的四年中，我们共同为美国人民提供了服务：缩小数字鸿沟；从地面5G到太空宽带，促进创新和竞争；保护消费者；和促进公共安全。
 我感到骄傲的是，该委员会的效率卓著，从四年内开始五次频谱拍卖和两次农村宽带反向拍卖，到开放1,245兆赫的中频频谱用于未经许可的使用，再到我们的《现代化媒体监管计划》通过了超过25份订单，以积极保护我们的通信网络免受国内外的国家安全威胁，将988指定为“国家自杀预防生命线”的三位数号码，等等。
 排在榜单上的尤为明显的是，排先生不幸的巨著，可以说是使他获得了这份工作的努力：取消了2015年的净中立规则。颠覆宽带公司的这些流行而重要的限制措施的最大胆的是不诚实和有党派的运动，一开始就使Pai的任期发了臭，因为没有任何好的工作可以洗掉。
  与往常一样，FCC的大部分职责在公众的视线范围之内，而在Pai之下以及在其他任何政府的领导下都进行了大量工作，这是无形的和令人费解的。 （尽管在某些情况下不像以前那样不可见-Pai的FCC确实以某种方式对透明度进行了改进。）
 正如他经常说的那样，排先生最重要的当然是改善“数字鸿沟”，这种鸿沟阻止了数百万的美国人享受负担得起的快速互联网。为了改善这种情况，已经创建了许多新的计划和资金，但是Pai受到不良信息（基本上由ISP自己在荣誉系统上提供）以及似乎无休止地推出5G的困扰，我们都还在等待。
final，他的最后努力不会太大地改善他的意见。当特朗普无礼地抨击第230条（该条使互联网公司免于用户行为承担责任的法律）时，Pai举起了那面旗帜，宣布他打算在选举前一个月重新审视并可能更改其解释。这项努力的愚蠢，明显的政治性质（几乎可以肯定现在将完全中止）引起了相当多的批评，这使本来已经令人不安的故事中的最后一章很糟糕。
 FCC的下一步是提名和确认新的董事长和替代委员会委员，尽管政治内部人士浮出了几个名字，但继承人显然还没有人出现。
note_en:
It is traditional at the FCC for the chairman to leave when the administration changes parties. Pai took over when Tom Wheeler, who chaired the commission at the end of the Obama years, resigned upon Trump’s election. The Biden administration has not announced its pick for the new leader of the communications agency.
 In an official FCC memo, Pai thanked his colleagues and summarized the accomplishments of his four years at the helm (as, it must also be said, the first commissioner of Asian descent):
  Together, we’ve delivered for the American people over the past four years: closing the digital divide; promoting innovation and competition, from 5G on the ground to broadband from space; protecting consumers; and advancing public safety.
 I am proud of how productive this commission has been, from commencing five spectrum auctions and two rural broadband reverse auctions in four years, to opening 1,245 megahertz of midband spectrum for unlicensed use, to adopting more than 25 orders through our Modernization of Media Regulation Initiative, to aggressively protecting our communications networks from national security threats at home and abroad, to designating 988 as the three-digit number for the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline, and much, much more.
 Notably absent from that list is Pai’s unfortunate magnum opus and arguably the effort that got him the job: The elimination of 2015’s net neutrality rules. The tremendously dishonest and partisan campaign to overturn these popular and important curbs on broadband companies put a stink on Pai’s tenure at the outset that no amount of good work could wash out.
  For as always, the bulk of the FCC’s duties fly under the public’s radar, and a great deal of work  was done under Pai, as under any other administration, invisibly and thanklessly. (Though in some cases less invisibly than before — Pai’s FCC did make improvements to transparency,  in some ways anyhow.)
 Surely Pai’s greatest priority was, as indeed he often stated, ameliorating the “digital divide” that prevents millions of Americans from enjoying affordable, fast internet. Numerous new programs and funds were created to improve this situation, but Pai was hampered by bad information —  essentially provided on the honor system by ISPs themselves — and the seemingly endless rollout of 5G, which we’re all still waiting on.
 His final effort, alas, will not much improve the opinion of him at large. As Trump raged impotently about Section 230, a law that shields internet companies from liability for the actions of their users, Pai took up that flag and  announced his intention to revisit and perhaps change the interpretation of it — a month before the election. The simpering, plainly political nature of the effort, almost certain now to be aborted entirely, attracted considerable criticism, makes for a poor final chapter in an already troubling story.
 The next step for the FCC is the nomination and confirmation of a new chairman and replacement commissioners, and though several names have been floated by political insiders, no one has emerged yet as the heir apparent.
title: FCC主席Ajit Pai将下台，为拜登政府腾出空间
title_en: FCC Chairman Ajit Pai will step down to make way for the Biden administration

note:
Wong在Twitter应用程序中启用了私人测试版，并截取了截图，显示了Audio Spaces及其功能的实际效果。当然，这些功能可能会在以后向公众推出之前进行更改，但是它提供了有关Twitter当前如何考虑该产品的想法。
  图像显示用户也可以将与今天可用于推文的对话控件同样应用于Audio Spaces。这将允许用户将其音频空间配置为对任何想加入的人，仅对他们关注的人或对他们特别邀请的人开放。
 用户也可以通过多种方式邀请他人加入自己的空间，包括通过DM（直接消息），发布推文或复制可以在其他地方共享的链接。
 当加入一个空间时，人们将在禁用麦克风的情况下进入空间以限制噪音。随着对话的进行，他们可以用各种各样的表情符号来回应所表达的表情，例如“ 100”，举手，拳头，和平标志和挥手。
 此外，音频空间的创建者将可以在创建专用房间后随时调整谁可以讲话。通过应用内菜单，他们将能够管理扬声器，调整其他设置，查看规则以及共享反馈或报告空间等。
  一个有趣的发现是，根据此菜单，“音频空间”将包含聊天记录。与其他一些音频聊天室服务相比，这是一个与众不同的功能。虽然表面上是为可访问性设计的功能，但它也可以使对话保持适当和尊重，这很有用，因为用户会知道自己的单词被写下来。
 这可能有助于解决私人聊天室模型中的一个问题，在该模型中，实时对话已被证明难以主持。例如，尽管处于接受邀请的Beta版中，但Clubhouse却已经经历了几次适度失败的事件，包括骚扰《纽约时报》记者的骚扰以及深入探讨反犹太主义的另一场对话。
多年来一直在努力打击其平台上的滥用行为的Twitter，在测试这种未经证实的在线社交新格式方面是一个值得怀疑的地方。
  尚不清楚Twitter如何在这些音频聊天室中进行审核，但似乎转录功能可以阻止有毒语音，而应用内报告功能则可以更直接地解决出现的问题。当用户选择“报告此空间”选项时，他们可以选择报告各种类别的内容，包括自我伤害，暴力，性内容，儿童安全，私人信息或虐待行为。
 由于Audio Spaces处于私用Beta版，因此测试人员还可以使用“共享反馈”选项，该选项允许他们将帐户@TwitterSpaces DM。
 Wong还指出，根据她对应用程序代码的挖掘，Audio Spaces正在将Periscope用作其后端。
 Twitter早些时候表示，Audio Spaces将向一小部分用户推出。在测试过程中，这些用户将包括一群“在平台上受到滥用和伤害的影响最大的群体：妇女和边缘化背景的人，” Twitter的产品设计师Maya Gold Patterson在为简报介绍该功能时指出。本月记者。
 Twitter发言人获得评论时说：“在公开Beta测试发布之前，我们将在Spaces上分享更多信息。”
note_en:
Wong enabled the private beta in the Twitter app and took screenshots that show how Audio Spaces and its features would look in action. Of course, these features could change before the feature later rolls out to the public, but it gives an idea about how Twitter is currently thinking about the product.
  The images show that users will be able to apply the same sort of conversation controls  that are today available for tweets to Audio Spaces, as well. This will allow users to configure their Audio Space to be open to anyone who wants to join, only to people they follow, or only to people they specifically invite to join.
 Users can invite others to their Space in a number of ways, too, including via DM (direct message), by posting a tweet, or copying a link that can be shared elsewhere.
 When joining a space, people will enter the space with their microphone disabled to limit noise. As the conversation progresses, they can react to what’s being said with a variety of emoji, like the “100,” raised hand, fist, peace sign, and waving hand.
 In addition, the Audio Space’s creator will be able to adjust who can speak at any time after the dedicated room has been created. From an in-app menu, they’ll be able to manage the speakers, adjust other settings, view the rules, as well as share feedback or report the space, among other things.
  One interesting finding is that Audio Spaces will include transcriptions of the chat, according to this menu. That’s a differentiating feature, compared with some other audio chat room services. While ostensibly a feature designed for accessibility, it could also prove useful in keeping the conversations appropriate and respectful, since users would know their words were being written down.
 This could help address one issue with the private chat room model, where live conversations have proven to be hard to moderate. Despite being in an invite-only beta, Clubhouse, for example, already experienced a handful of incidents of moderation failure, including the  harassment of a New York Times reporter and another conversation that  delved into anti-Semitism.
 Twitter, which has struggled for years to combat abuse on its platform, was a questionable place to be testing this unproven new format for online socializing.
  It wasn’t clear how Twitter would be approach moderation for these audio chat rooms, but it appears the transcription feature could a deterrent to toxic speech while the in-app reporting feature allows for a more direct solution to problems that crop up. When users choose the “Report this Space” option, they can then choose to report across a variety of categories, including self-harm, violence, sexual content, child safety, private information or abusive behavior.
 Because Audio Spaces is in private beta, testers will also have access to a “Share Feedback” option that allows them to DM the account @TwitterSpaces.
 Wong also noted Audio Spaces is using Periscope for its backend, according to her digging in the app’s code.
 Twitter earlier said Audio Spaces would be launching to a small group of users. During tests, those users would include a group of people who are “disproportionately impacted by abuse and harm on the platform: women and those from marginalized backgrounds,” Twitter Staff Product Designer Maya Gold Patterson had noted, when introducing the feature in a briefing for reporters this month.
 Reached for comment, a Twitter spokesperson said: “We’ll have more to share on Spaces closer to the public beta experiment launch.”
title: Twitter的Audio Spaces测试包括转录，说话者控制和报告功能
title_en: Twitter’s Audio Spaces test includes transcriptions, speaker controls, and reporting features

note:
C3.ai的初始发行价区间为每股31美元至34美元，该公司预计将以该价格出售1550万股。这家以企业为中心的人工智能公司还以IPO价格向Spring Creek Capital出售了1亿美元的股票，以相同的价格向微软出售了5000万美元。并为承销商保留了232.5万股股票。
 C3.ai的IPO股份出售后，Spring Creek和Microsoft买入，承销商承购的股票总数为99,216,958。在其初始IPO价格区间的极端值下，按照该股数计算，该公司的市值将在30.8亿美元至33.7亿美元之间。
 如果承销商不购买他们的期权，这些数字将分别减少约70美元和8000万美元。
 那么，以这些价格进行的首次公开募股是公司的胜利吗？对所有C3.ai投资者来说都是胜利吗？令人惊讶的是，感觉答案是肯定的。让我们探讨一下原因。
  如果只看C3.ai的收入历史，就可以为公司的成长故事辩护。收入从截至7月31日的2019年第二季度的7380万美元增长到2020年同期的8180万美元。同比增长不到11％。不是很好，但是很积极。
note_en:
C3.ai posted an initial IPO price range of $31 to $34 per share, with the company anticipating a sale of 15.5 million shares at that price. The enterprise-focused artificial intelligence company is also selling $100 million of stock at its IPO price to Spring Creek Capital, and another $50 million to Microsoft at the same terms. And there are 2.325 million shares reserved for its underwriters as well.
 The total tally of shares that C3.ai will have outstanding after its IPO bloc is sold, Spring Creek and Microsoft buy in, and its underwriters take up their option, is 99,216,958. At the extremes of its initial IPO price range, the company would be worth between $3.08 billion and $3.37 billion using that share count.
 Those numbers decline by around $70 and $80 million, respectively, if the underwriters do not purchase their option.
 So is the IPO a win for the company at those prices? And is it a win for  all C3.ai investors? Amazingly enough, it feels like the answers are yes and no. Let’s explore why.
  If we just look at C3.ai’s revenue history in chunks, you can argue a growth story for the company; that it grew from $73.8 million in the the two quarters of 2019 ending July 31, to $81.8 million in revenue during the same portion of 2020. That’s growth of just under 11% on a year-over-year basis. Not great, but positive.
title: C3.ai的初始IPO定价指导凸显了公众市场的技术胃口
title_en: C3.ai’s initial IPO pricing guidance spotlights the public market’s tech appetite

  硅谷移民律师索菲·阿尔康（Sophie Alcorn）表示：“您的问题对于知识的传播至关重要，知识的传播使全世界的人们都能跨越国界，实现自己的梦想。” “无论您是人来人往，是创始人还是正在硅谷谋职，我都希望在下一篇专栏中回答您的问题。”
 Extra Crunch成员可以访问每周的“亲爱的苏菲”专栏；使用促销代码ALCORN以50％的折扣购买为期一年或两年的订阅。
    非常感谢您在今年的整个过程中成为“亲爱的苏菲”的一员。当我回顾感恩节假期的周末时，我很感激2020年全世界所有人的认识。无论身在何处或居住地，我们都相互联系。今年包括重大的变革性事件。这些变化使我们能够更好地了解我们想要什么和不需要什么。因此，我坚信我们的未来经验将得到改善。
 回顾去年，我对特朗普总统为改善H-1B彩票流程所做的数字化工作表示赞赏。
 展望未来，它是令人振奋的是增加获得移民机会是总统当选人拜登的一大重点。我相信，拜登·哈里斯（Biden-Harris）政府将支持美国将我们的根基视为机遇之地。进入2021年，我们将认识到我们的移民遗产，欢迎新移民，并认识到移民对改善世界的重要贡献。
  我感谢您，我的读者以及从您那里收到的有关本专栏，您所遇到的问题和希望看到的话题的信息和反馈。我非常感谢TechCrunch和Extra Crunch在这个平台上分享我的想法，经验和知识。
我非常感谢来自世界各地的所有成功支持我们的客户。今年的许多时刻似乎令人沮丧，但我们还是能够度过的。我赞赏他们为美国做出的许多贡献，并创造了健康解决方案和创造就业机会，因为他们继续在硅谷及其他地区创办和扩展创新型初创公司。
 我非常感谢Alcorn Immigration Law出色的团队，并感谢我们在支持人们到美国生活和工作并实现梦想方面所取得的成功。我很感谢我们的团队编写“向移民律师提出的64个问题”，这是一份在开始移民程序之前与移民律师面谈时应该问的问题清单。我很高兴有机会在我的播客“技术创业公司移民法”中分享我的知识-本周的播客都是关于感谢！
 最后，我对自己的出色工作表示赞赏。我有幸支持来自世界各地的人们创造他们的梦想。听客户的故事，希望和梦想简直令人振奋。这是最出色的国际象棋游戏，用于识别和定制最适合其独特情况，优先事项和时机的移民策略。
 成为一名移民律师的原因之一，是因为我们出色的客户委托我们来支持他们在美国移民系统中导航，以实现他们的梦想。今年，我们取得了许多重大法律胜利：
 我感谢以雇员身份获得E-2条约投资者签证的客户。他迫切希望加入一家早期阶段的初创公司，并陷入困境，需要在大流行中迅速获得批准，并能够向其当前雇主提供合同通知。我们都知道这是冒险的，所以我为我们的团队成功地向这家初创公司请愿他以O-1A签证获得非凡能力身份而赞助他感到自豪。
 我也感谢有抱负的创业者创始人，我们通过协助他自我呈请绿卡来帮助他从公司雇主中获得独立。我们成功地使他获得了EB-2 NIW（国家免税）卓越能力绿卡的批准。
 我也很感激我们成功支持了一个潜在的初创公司联合创始人留在美国，同时保持了他的绿卡地位。一位著名的风险投资家要求他立即离开现任雇主，并在投资600万美元之前就开始为这家初创公司全职工作。该创始人曾在L-1A Visa的先前公司中受过公司内部受让人管理人员和高管的约束，并且他不想失去自己的中间绿卡流程。我们成功地将他迅速转移到新公司，并确保了他的绿卡可移植性。现在，他可以专注于创业和与家人共度时光。
尽管大多数美国领事馆仍处于关闭状态，但我很高兴我们能够支持我们的客户进行E-3签证面试，获得她的签证批准，即使在大流行期间，也能够移居美国。
 值得注意的是，我们帮助客户避免了在其J-1教育和文化交流签证即将到期且雇主即将进行一轮裁员之后的两年内不必回国。我们指导她完成了绿卡程序，包括帮助她准备在美国公民及移民服务局（USCIS）进行的面试，以及陪同她进行面试。现在，她没有被驱逐出美国，而是在庆祝这是她的永久居所。
  我也很感激我们开设了首个在线移民课程“非凡能力训练营”。我们的许多客户成功来自诸如O-1A非移民签证，EB-1A非凡能力绿卡和EB-2 NIW绿卡之类的选择。我很高兴有机会录制一系列课程，可以帮助任何人达到美国移民的标准。
 感恩节，我希望您能体会到这种对人生中的人和经历的欣赏之情。我对未来充满热情，并渴望看到未来。 2020年告诉我，我们现在有能力，因为我们可以自由选择自己的感受。我们总是可以选择无条件地爱和欣赏。即将到来的新机遇将为我们所有人提供支持。
     有一个问题？在这里问。我们保留编辑您提交内容的权利，以确保内容清晰和/或清晰。 “亲爱的苏菲”中提供的信息是一般信息，而非法律建议。有关“亲爱的苏菲”的限制的更多信息，请在此处查看我们的完整免责声明。您可以直接通过Alcorn移民法与Sophie联系。
 苏菲（Sophie）的播客（适用于技术创业公司的移民法）可在所有主要播客平台上使用。如果您想成为客人，她正在接受申请！
note_en:
Sophie Alcorn is the founder of  Alcorn Immigration Law in Silicon Valley and 2019 Global Law Experts Awards’ “Law Firm of the Year in California for Entrepreneur Immigration Services.” She connects people with the businesses and opportunities that expand their lives.
  “Your questions are vital to the spread of knowledge that allows people all over the world to rise above borders and pursue their dreams,” says  Sophie Alcorn, a Silicon Valley immigration attorney. “Whether you’re in people ops, a founder or seeking a job in Silicon Valley, I would love to  answer your questions in my next column.”
 Extra Crunch members receive access to weekly “Dear Sophie” columns;  use promo code ALCORN to purchase a one- or two-year subscription for 50% off.
    Thank you so much for being a part of the genesis of “Dear Sophie” over the course of this year. As I reflect on the Thanksgiving holiday weekend, I’m appreciative of how much all of us around the world have come to know in 2020. We are all interconnected, regardless of where we were born or wherever we currently reside. This year has included major, transformative events. These changes serve us to better know what we want and what we don’t. As a result, I am positive that our future experiences will be enhanced.
 Looking back over the last year, I’m appreciative of President Trump’s digitization effort to improve the H-1B lottery process.
 Looking forward, it’s exhilarating that increasing access to immigration opportunities is a major priority for President-elect Biden. I’m confident the Biden-Harris administration will support the U.S. embracing our roots as a land of opportunity. Moving into 2021 we will recognize our immigrant heritage, welcome newcomers and recognize the important contributions of immigrants for a better world.
  I’m appreciative of you, my readers, and the messages and feedback I receive from you about this column, questions you have and topics you would like to see covered. I appreciate TechCrunch and Extra Crunch for this platform to share my thoughts, experiences and knowledge.
 I’m appreciative of all of our clients from around the world who we’ve been able to successfully support. Many moments this year seemed bleak, but we were able to come through. I appreciate their many contributions to the U.S. and creating health solutions and jobs as they have gone on to launch and scale innovative startups in Silicon Valley and beyond.
 I’m appreciative of my amazing team at Alcorn Immigration Law and for our successes in supporting folks to come to live and work in the U.S. and achieve their dreams. And I’m appreciative of our team to compile a “ 64 Questions to Ask Your Immigration Attorney,” a checklist of questions you should ask when interviewing immigration attorneys before starting the immigration process. I’m appreciative for having the opportunity to share my knowledge on my podcast, Immigration Law for Tech Startups — this week’s  podcast is all about appreciation!
 And finally, I’m appreciative of my amazing job. I have the privilege of supporting people from all around the world to create their dreams. It’s humbling and inspiring to listen to my clients’ stories, hopes and dreams. It’s the most magnificent chess game to identify and tailor immigration strategies that best fit their unique situation, priorities and timing.
 Part of why being an immigration attorney inspires me is because our amazing clients entrust us to support them in navigating the U.S. immigration system to make their dream a reality. We had many major legal victories this year:
 I appreciate the client who was on an  E-2 Visa for Treaty Investors as an employee. He was desperate to join an early-stage startup and was in a difficult bind of needing to get expedited approval in the pandemic and be able to provide his contractual notice to his current employer. We all knew it was risky, so I’m proud of our team for successfully petitioning for the startup to sponsor him in  O-1A Visa for Extraordinary Ability status.
 I also appreciate the aspiring startup founder we helped to gain independence from a corporate employer by assisting him with self-petitioning his green card. We succeeded in getting him approved for an  EB-2 NIW (National Interest Waiver) exceptional ability green card.
 I am also appreciating that we successfully supported a prospective startup co-founder to remain in the U.S. while maintaining his position in line for a green card. A prominent VC required that he immediately leave his current employer and begin working full time for the very early-stage startup prior to investing $6 million. This founder had been bound at a prior company in  L-1A Visa for Intracompany Transferee Managers and Executives, and he didn’t want to lose his midstream green card process. We successfully transitioned him to the new company quickly and secured him green card portability. He can now focus on the startup and spending time with his family.
 While most U.S. consulates remained closed, I appreciate that we were able to support our client to get an  E-3 Visa interview, have her visa approved and be able to move to the U.S., even in the middle of the pandemic.
 Notably, we helped a client avoid having to return to her home country for  two years after her  J-1 Educational and Cultural Exchange Visa was set to expire, and her employer was about to do a round of layoffs. We guided her through the green card process, including helping her prepare for an interview at U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS), as well as accompanying her to the interview. Instead of being banished from the U.S., now she is celebrating that it is her permanent home.
  I’m also appreciative that we launched our first online immigration course,  Extraordinary Ability Bootcamp. Many of our client successes stem from options such as the O-1A nonimmigrant visa, as well as the  EB-1A extraordinary ability green card and the EB-2 NIW green card. I’m grateful to have had the opportunity to record a series of classes that can help anybody meet the criteria for U.S. immigration.
 This Thanksgiving, I hope you caught a glimpse of this feeling of appreciation for people and experiences in your life. I feel exhilarated and eager about the future and to see what’s ahead. 2020 has taught me that we are empowered at this moment because we have the freedom to choose how we feel. We can always choose to love and appreciate unconditionally. New opportunities are ahead that will support us all.
     Have a question? Ask it here. We reserve the right to edit your submission for clarity and/or space. The information provided in “Dear Sophie” is general information and not legal advice. For more information on the limitations of “Dear Sophie,” please view our full disclaimer  here. You can contact Sophie directly at Alcorn Immigration Law.
 Sophie’s podcast,  Immigration Law for Tech Startups, is available on all major podcast platforms. If you’d like to be a guest,  she’s accepting applications!

新的启动窗口为12月19日（太平洋标准时间上午10点至下午2点）。该公司表示，还有一个备份窗口设置为12月20日，时间跨度类似，该公司表示，以及接下来几周的备份时间，以防出于某种原因需要重新安排备份时间。该演示将涉及整个维京轨道发射系统的完整发射周期，包括其“宇宙女孩”（Cosmic Girl）发射飞机（经改装的747客机）和“ LauncherOne”，这是一种在巡航高度从宇宙女孩分离的火箭，然后自行燃烧以制造发动机。太空旅行的其余部分均携带小型卫星有效载荷。
 Virgin Orbit的系统之所以独特，是因为它可以从传统的机场起飞和降落，从而无需专门的发射场，并释放了相对低空飞行的全球发射灵活性的潜力。它也有可能为希望仅发射一枚或几枚航天器的小型卫星公司提供成本和调度优势，而不必等待诸如SpaceX的大型火箭的乘车共享任务的时间安排或为某些事情支付溢价就像Rocket Lab的产品一样。
  上一次在五月左右，维珍轨道号的飞行从起飞到将LauncherOne与舰载飞机分离开来，一切都非常完美。火箭甚至按计划按时点火启动发动机，但是由于内置的​​安全系统在检测到一些异常读数时也按计划工作，因此发动机基本上立即停机。
  通过第二次尝试，Virgin Orbit希望表明从那时起，它的系统也可以正常运行，并具有完整的第一级动力飞行和较高级的操作。这次的赌注会更高一些，因为在船上将是实际的客户卫星，尽管从技术上讲这仍然是一个示范任务，其主要目的是收集数据。
 机载的10个有效载荷来自NASA，代表了完全由美国的大学和学术机构创建的许多不同的科学和教育计划。
note_en:
The new launch window is December 19, between the hours of 10 AM to 2 PM PST. There’s also a backup window set for December 20 ranging across similar hours, the company says, and others in the following weeks, in case it needs to be rescheduled for nay reason. This demonstration will involve a full launch cycle of the entire Virgin Orbit launch system, including its Cosmic Girl launch aircraft (a modiified 747 passenger airliner) and LauncherOne, the rocket that detaches from Cosmic Girl at cruising altitude before firing up its own engines to make the rest of the trip to space with small satellite payloads on board.
 Virgin Orbit’s system is unique because it takes off and lands from a traditional airport, eliminating the need for specialized launch sites and opening up the potential of relatively low-lift global launch flexibility. It also have the potential to offer cost and scheduling advantages to small satellite companies looking to launch just one or a few spacecraft, without having to wait for timing on a rideshare mission on a larger rocket like one from  SpaceX,   or pay a premium for something like Rocket Lab’s offering.
  Last time around in May, Virgin Orbit’s flight went perfectly from takeoff through the separation of LauncherOne from the carrier aircraft. The rocket even fired up its engines on time as planned, but the engines cut off essentially right away due to a built-in safety system that also worked as planned when it detected some unusual readings.
  With this second attempt, Virgin Orbit wants to show that it’s system works from that point on, as well, with a full first-stage powered flight, and operation of the upper stage. Stakes are a bit higher this time around, as on board will be actual customer satellites, even though this is technically still a demonstration mission the primary purpose of which is to collect data.
 The 10 payloads on board are from NASA, and represent a number of different scientific and educational programs created entirely by U.S.-based universities and academic institutions.

note:
Gainsight和首席执行官尼克·梅塔（Nick Mehta）花费了十年的大部分时间，努力将“客户成功”作为软件类别进行推广，试图成为其第一家价值10亿美元的创业公司。现在，Gainsight达到了这一估值-但通过将其业务的控股权出售给了私募股权巨头。
  Vista Equity Partners已经收购了Gainsight的多数股权，该交易对这家总部位于旧金山的公司的估值为11亿美元。 Gainsight此前已经筹集了超过1.5亿美元的风险投资，其中包括2017年的5200万美元E轮投资。
 在确认收购的采访中，Mehta表示，与Vista的谈判在10月份激增，因为该公司还与银行家就可能在未来几个财政季度进行的IPO展开探索性谈判。该公司拒绝提供收入，但梅赫塔表示，Gainsight已从Covid-19大流行期间的业务趋势中受益，有望实现其2020年底的年度经常性收入约为1亿美元的目标。
 Gainsight的坚实成果仍然与一些内部人士和支持者对公司的期望持平。事实证明，该公司在建立社区方面更为成功（今年的主要活动吸引了23,000名从业人员），而不是实现“高速增长”。梅塔（Mehta）表示，Gainsight在三年前进行了最后一次主要募捐之后，就决定优先考虑可持续性，并且在最近几个月提高了利润率。
 “每个市场都有自己的自然增长率。如果您想长期存在，那么以对那个市场有意义的速度增长非常重要。” Mehta说。 “随着公司转向订阅和云计算，我们的业务不断增长，在可预见的未来，这将持续下去。但是在任何一家公司中，这都不是一朝一夕的事。”
 借助Vista，Gainsight获得了一个控制合作伙伴，该伙伴已成为挑选可靠的软件业务并进行交易或稍后公开上市的专家。去年10月，它公开收购了另一项先前的收购，即数据软件业务Datto，加入了包括Ping Security和JAMF在内的近期IPO。 11月，Vista收购了一家与Gainsight业务相邻的客户关系管理公司Pipedrive的多数股权。 （Vista创始人兼亿万富翁罗伯特·史密斯（Robert Smith）最近也因其他原因成为头条新闻，以1.4亿美元和解了一项联邦逃税案，并承认有不法行为。）
 该公司最初由Jim Eberlin和Sreedhar Peddineni于2009年以JBara Software的身份成立，并引入了梅塔（Mehta），后者将初创公司LiveOffice卖给了赛门铁克（Symantec），并于2013年更名为Gainsight。梅塔在一次采访中说，“梅塔将球一直踢到末尾区99码。”
收购后，一些投资者仍持有Gainsight的股份。李说，早期投资者更有可能兑现。他拒绝评论Battery Ventures是否仍持有股票。 Lee评估了此次交易的结果，认为定义新类别的初创公司需要更多的精力和时间来构建。他说：“然后，您回顾后视镜，这是十年来成功的经典故事。”
 Mehta在五次成为Cloud 100榜单榜首的Gainsight上说，他将自己的“很多”钱与Gainsight的成功发展联系在一起，没有计划离开。 Gainsight计划用这笔资金增加新产品和并购，而IPO仍是其最终目标。
 “我觉得过去七年半，我们已经爬上了一座山，真是太棒了。这真是艰辛而艰巨，充满挑战和乐趣，”梅赫塔说。 “现在，我们想去爬更大的山，嘿，我们拥有世界上最好的夏尔巴协作，可以帮助我们到达那里。”
note_en:
Gainsight and CEO Nick Mehta spent the better part of a decade working to popularize “customer success” as a software category, attempting to become its first billion-dollar startup. Now, Gainsight is reaching that valuation—but by selling a controlling interest of its business to a private equity giant.
  Vista Equity Partners has acquired a majority stake of Gainsight in a deal that values the San Francisco-based company at $1.1 billion. Gainsight had previously raised more than $150 million in venture capital, including  $52 million in a Series E investment in 2017.
 In an interview confirming the acquisition, Mehta said talks with Vista heated up in October as the company also opened exploratory talks with bankers about a potential IPO a few fiscal quarters down the road. The company declined to provide its revenue, but Mehta said Gainsight had benefited from business trends during the Covid-19 pandemic, on track to meet its 2020 end-of-year target of about $100 million in annualized recurring revenue.
 Gainsight’s solid outcome still falls flat of what some insiders and supporters would have hoped for the company, which proved more successful at building a community—its main event this year drew 23,000 practitioner attendees—than achieving “hyper growth.” Mehta said Gainsight had decided three years ago, after its last major fundraise, to prioritize sustainability, and had improved its margins in recent months.
 “Every market has its own natural growth rate. If you want to be around for a long time, it’s important to actually grow at the rate that makes sense for that market,” Mehta said. “Our business grows as companies move to subscriptions and the cloud, and that’s a wave that’s going to keep going for the foreseeable future. But in any one company it doesn’t just happen overnight.”
 With Vista, Gainsight gains a controlling partner that has become something of a specialist at picking up solid software businesses and flipping them or taking them public later. In October,  it took public another previous acquisition, data software business Datto, joining recent IPOs including Ping Security and JAMF. And in November, Vista  took a majority stake in a customer relationship management company adjacent to Gainsight’s business, Pipedrive. (Vista founder and billionaire Robert Smith also recently made headlines for other reasons,  settling a federal tax evasion case for $140 million and admitting wrongdoing.)
 Originally founded in 2009 by Jim Eberlin and Sreedhar Peddineni as JBara Software, the company brought in Mehta, who had sold startup LiveOffice to Symantec, as CEO and rebranded as Gainsight in 2013. Roger Lee, one of the company’s first investors at Battery Ventures, said in an interview that Mehta “took the ball 99 yards all the way to the end zone.”
  Some investors maintained stakes in Gainsight after the acquisition; Lee said early investors were more likely to have cashed out. He declined to comment on whether Battery Ventures still held shares. Assessing the sale’s outcome, Lee argued that startups that define new categories take more effort and time to build. “Then you look back in the rearview mirror, and it’s a classic story of ten-year overnight success,” he said.
 At Gainsight, a five-time Cloud 100  list-maker, Mehta says he tied “a lot” of his own money to Gainsight’s success moving forward, with no plans to depart. Gainsight plans to add new products and eye acquisitions with the funds, with an IPO still its eventual goal.
 “I feel like the last seven and a half years, we’ve climbed a mountain, and it’s been awesome. It’s been really hard and challenging, and fun and rewarding,” Mehta says. “Now we want to go climb a bigger mountain, and hey, we have the best Sherpas in the world to get us to it.”

Bitcoin surged Monday to set its first fresh record in nearly three years, driven by a wave of new investors lured by the potential for big profits.
  The digital currency rose as high as $19,786.24, up 8.4% on the day. That topped the previous intraday record of $19,783.21 set Dec. 18, 2017, according to CoinDesk. Bitcoin has nearly tripled in 2020 and is up 97% since early September. Most recently, it traded at $19,283.
title: 比特币价格创下历史新高19,786美元，创2017年新高
title_en: Bitcoin Price Hits All-Time High of $19,786, Topping 2017 Record
